Known Issues (easiest to hardest to fix)
==========================================

1. [SOLVED] Item freezes when dropped onto a grid smaller than the item
   Clamp calls in Grid fail when the grid is narrower or shorter than the item
   being dragged, because max becomes less than min. The item gets stuck and
   throws two errors. Fixing it requires guarding the clamp max values with
   math.max(0, ...) and adding an IsRegionInBounds check before finalizing the
   drop position. The exact lines to change are clearly identified and the patch
   is straightforward.

2. [SOLVED] Items are invisible while dragging inside a ClipsDescendants GuiObject
   When a grid lives inside a frame with ClipsDescendants enabled the item
   element is clipped and disappears the moment it is dragged outside the frame
   boundary. Re-parenting the dragged item element to a top-level ScreenGui
   while it is being dragged and returning it afterwards would resolve this.

3. [SOLVED] Single slot appears completely white and is non-functional after setup
   The slot canvas group renders as solid white instead of showing the expected
   background. This is likely an initialisation ordering problem where the slot
   element properties are applied before the GuiObject has fully entered the
   DataModel. Ensuring the slot element is added to the parent before setting
   visual properties should fix it.

4. [SOLVED] Images display incorrectly inside single slots
   Item images are offset or scaled wrongly when placed inside a single slot.
   The sizing logic for single slots does not account for the image label
   position correctly. Adjusting the image scaling/anchoring code that is
   specific to the single-slot path is all that is needed.

5. [SOLVED] Spam-clicking and rotating lets items pass through other items or escape the grid
   Rapid click-and-rotate input causes the drop handler to run before the
   collision/bounds state has fully updated, allowing an item to be placed on
   top of another item or outside the grid boundary. Adding a debounce or a
   lock flag around the drop finalization logic would prevent the race
   condition.

6. [SOLVED] Improper positioning and scale of items created while a parent ancestor is hidden
   AbsoluteSize and AbsolutePosition return zero when any ancestor has Visible
   set to false or when a ScreenGui is disabled, so item elements are sized and
   placed incorrectly. The fix involves listening for the ancestor becoming
   visible and recalculating layout at that point, or falling back to Size
   offset values when absolute values are unavailable.

7. [SOLVED] No built-in way to restrict which item types a single slot or grid will accept
   There is currently no blacklist or whitelist mechanism on ItemManagers.
   Users have to duplicate filtering logic every time they connect a
   TransferLink. Adding an optional filter callback to each ItemManager (similar
   to MoveMiddleware on items) would solve this and requires extending the
   ItemManager API surface.

8. [SOLVED] No MoveMiddleware equivalent for ItemManagers
   Items support a MoveMiddleware callback that can intercept and cancel a move
   before it happens, but no equivalent hook exists on the receiving
   ItemManager. Implementing one requires designing the callback signature,
   threading the call through the move pipeline, and documenting the new API.

9. [SOLVED] Drag detection does not work on touch or gamepad devices
   The current implementation hooks mouse input events and an interaction
   button, so dragging does not work on non-mouse platforms. UIDragDetectors
   would provide native multi-platform drag support and simplify the backend considerably.
   This requires a significant custom input abstraction layer.

10. Inventories are entirely client-managed with no server authority
    All grid and item state lives on the client, making it difficult to persist
    data reliably, prevent cheating, or share inventory state across clients.
    Moving to a server-managed architecture would require rewriting the core
    data model, introducing remote events or attributes for synchronisation,
    and handling the latency that comes with every interaction. The added server
    authority improves security and simplifies data persistence, but it
    introduces input latency that must be carefully managed to keep interactions
    feeling responsive. This is the most invasive change and would introduce
    breaking API changes for all existing users.
